title: 10 best herbal creams for sleep deprivation

remedy_properties: Sedative and relaxing effects: Valerian root contains compounds that interact with GABA receptors in the brain, promoting relaxation, reducing anxiety and stress, and helping to fall asleep faster.
Anxiolytic (anti-anxiety) properties: The sedative effect of valerian root also helps reduce anxiety and worry, making it easier to calm down before bed and stay asleep throughout the night.
Muscle relaxant properties: Valerian cream can help soothe muscle tension and cramps, which are common causes of insomnia, allowing for a deeper and more restful sleep.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valeriana officinalis, also known as valerian, creams helps with sleep deprivation because they contain valerenic acid and valepotriates, which have a sedative and calming effect on the mind and body. These compounds interact with the brain's neurotransmitters, such as GABA, to promote relaxation and reduce anxiety. As a result, valerian creams can help individuals fall asleep faster, sleep more soundly, and wake up feeling refreshed and rejuvenated, making them an effective natural remedy for people struggling with sleep deprivation.
remedy_constituents: Valerenic acid: This terpene has sedative properties, helping to reduce anxiety and promote relaxation, which can aid in falling asleep.
Isovaleric acid: This terpene has a sedative effect, reducing muscle tension and promoting a calming effect on the nervous system, making it easier to fall asleep.
Valeranone: This valerenic acid derivative has a sedative effect, acting on the GABA receptors in the brain, which can help reduce anxiety and promote relaxation, making it easier to fall asleep.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Valeriana officinalis contain a high concentration of valerenic acid, a key compound responsible for its sedative properties.
Stems: The stems of Valeriana officinalis contain valerenic acid and other bioactive compounds that contribute to its relaxing and sleep-inducing effects.
Barks: Valeriana officinalis bark contains valerenic acid and other compounds that are used to reduce anxiety and promote a restful night's sleep.
remedy_recipe: Extract 25 grams of dried valeriana officinalis root in 500 milliliters of carrier oil like sweet almond oil.
Steep the mixture at room temperature for 2-3 weeks shaking daily for optimal extraction.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into a clean bowl discarding the solids.
Combine 100 grams of the extracted liquid with 100 grams of beeswax and 50 grams of cocoa butter.
Melt the mixture in a double boiler then pour into containers for cooling and hardening.

remedy_properties: Sedative and calming effects: Lavender's sedative properties help to calm the mind and body, reducing anxiety and stress, making it easier to fall asleep.

Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties: Lavender's antioxidants and anti-inflammatory agents can help reduce inflammation in the body and brain, which is often linked to sleep deprivation, promoting a restful night's sleep.
Relaxation and reduced muscle tension: The calming effects of lavender can also help relax muscles and reduce physical tension, making it easier to fall asleep and stay asleep throughout the night.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia, also known as English lavender, creams helps with sleep deprivation because of its calming and soothing properties. The key active compounds, linalool and linalyl acetate, work to promote relaxation and reduce anxiety levels. This allows the body to unwind and prepare for restful sleep. The cooling sensation of the cream on the skin can also help to lower body temperature, a natural indicator of sleepiness. Regular use of Lavandula angustifolia creams can lead to improved sleep quality, helping to alleviate the strain of sleep deprivation.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: A terpene found in Lavandula angustifolia, linalool has a sedative effect on the nervous system, helping to reduce anxiety and promote relaxation, making it easier to fall asleep.
Linalyl acetate: A terpene ester also present in the herb, linalyl acetate has a calming effect on the mind and body, reducing stress and promoting a peaceful sleep environment.
Lavandulol: A terpene with sedative properties, lavandulol helps to regulate sleep patterns by reducing anxiety and promoting relaxation, making it easier to fall asleep and stay asleep.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are used due to their calming properties, which help to promote relaxation and reduce stress.
Flowers: Flowers are used for their sedative and anti-anxiety effects, which aid in inducing a restful sleep.
Seeds: Seeds are used for their calming and soothing properties, which help to reduce insomnia and promote better sleep quality.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers from a clean and dry environment to prevent contamination.
Mix the dried flowers with 50g of sweet almond oil in a double boiler to create a liquid solution.
Steep the mixture for 4 hours at a temperature of 70 degrees celsius to allow the herbal properties to infuse.
Strain the liquid solution through a cheesecloth into a clean container to separate the particles from the oil.
Whip 100g of distilled water with 20g of beeswax and the strained herbal solution to create a smooth and creamy texture.

remedy_properties: Anxiolytic (anti-anxiety) effects: Lemon balm cream's anxiolytic properties can help reduce anxiety levels, making it easier to fall asleep and stay asleep.
Muscle relaxant properties: The presence of rosmarinic acid in lemon balm cream can help relax muscles, reducing restlessness and promoting a sense of calm, making it easier to drift off to sleep.

Sedative effects: Lemon balm's sedative properties can help regulate the body's natural sleep-wake cycle, also known as the circadian rhythm, by increasing the levels of serotonin and melatonin in the brain.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Melissa officinalis, also known as lemon balm, creams helps with sleep deprivation because of its unique ability to calm the mind and relax the body. The antispasmodic and sedative properties of lemon balm help to soothe the nervous system, reducing anxiety and stress that can disrupt sleep. Additionally, lemon balm's ability to reduce inflammation and promote a sense of relaxation can help individuals fall asleep faster and stay asleep longer, leading to a restful night's sleep and improved overall well-being.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: As a phenolic compound, rosmarinic acid helps reduce stress and anxiety levels, promoting relaxation and improving sleep quality.
Citrals: The citral compounds present in Melissa officinalis have a sedative and calming effect, helping to regulate the body's sleep-wake cycle and reduce insomnia.
Linalool: A terpene found in Melissa officinalis, linalool has a soothing effect on the nervous system, reducing stress and anxiety, and promoting relaxation and deeper sleep.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in essential oils, Melissa leaves are commonly used to create sleep-promoting creams due to their calming and sedative properties.
Flowers: The flowers of Melissa officinalis are rich in apigenin, a natural compound that has a sedative effect on the nervous system, making them a popular choice for sleep creams.
Stems: The stems of Melissa officinalis contain a high concentration of essential oils, which are often used to create sleep-promoting creams that help to relax the mind and body.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20g of melissa officinalis leaves and flowers in a clean container on a sunny day.
Mix 20g of melissa officinalis leaves and flowers with 200ml of cold-pressed oil in a clean glass jar for 2 hours.
Steep the mixture in a double boiler at 60°c for 2 hours and 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container and discard the solids.
Add 50ml of distilled water and 10g of beeswax to the mixture and heat it in a double boiler until melted.

remedy_properties: Passionflower (Passiflora incarnata): Maypop creams often contain passionflower, which has a sedative effect on the nervous system, helping to calm anxiety and promote relaxation, making it easier to fall asleep.

Valerian root: Valerian root is another common ingredient in maypop creams that can help with sleep deprivation. It has a calming effect on the central nervous system, reducing stress and promoting relaxation, which can improve the quality of sleep.

Lavender oil: Lavender oil, often added to maypop creams, has a soothing and calming effect on the mind and body, reducing anxiety and promoting a sense of calmness, making it easier to fall asleep and stay asleep.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Passiflora incarnata, also known as maypop, creams helps with sleep deprivation because they contain high levels of the sleep-promoting compound passifloric acid. This herb has been traditionally used to calm the mind and body, reducing anxiety and stress that can disrupt sleep patterns. By applying a topical cream, the passifloric acid is absorbed into the skin, promoting relaxation and tranquility, making it easier to fall asleep and stay asleep throughout the night. This natural approach can help regulate sleep patterns and improve overall sleep quality.
remedy_constituents: Harmane: Harmane is a phenolic alkaloid that helps with sleep deprivation by acting as a central nervous system depressant, promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety.

Flavonoids (specifically isovitexin and isoorientin): These flavonoids have a sedative effect, helping to alleviate stress and anxiety by modulating the activity of neurotransmitters involved in sleep regulation.

Isovitexin-4'-O-glucoside (a flavonoid glycoside): This compound has been shown to possess sedative and anxiolytic properties, contributing to the relaxation and sleep-promoting effects of Passiflora incarnata creams.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They contain flavonoids and other compounds that have a sedative effect, contributing to relaxation and improved sleep.
Roots: The root extracts have a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help alleviate insomnia and other sleep disorders.
Seeds: The seeds contain a high concentration of harmine and other alkaloids, which have a sedative and sleep-promoting effect.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of passiflora incarnata flowers and leaves when they are in full bloom and dry them immediately.
Combine the dried passiflora incarnata with 100ml of carrier oil in a double boiler and heat it to 60c for 2 hours.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth and discard the solids then add 50g of beeswax to the oil mixture.
Melt the beeswax in the oil mixture over low heat and stir until it reaches a temperature of 40c.
Pour the mixture into small containers and allow it to cool and solidify at room temperature for 30 minutes.

remedy_properties: Alpha-Bisabolol: A natural anti-inflammatory compound that soothes skin irritations, reduces anxiety and stress, and promotes relaxation, making it easier to fall asleep.
Humulone: A sesquiterpene with sedative and calming properties, which can help reduce restlessness and promote a sense of calmness, leading to improved sleep quality.
Farnesol: An essential oil with anxiolytic and anticonvulsant properties, which can help alleviate anxiety and stress, promoting relaxation and reducing the time it takes to fall asleep.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Humulus lupulus, also known as hops, creams helps with sleep deprivation because of its natural sedative properties. The herb contains flavonoids and essential oils that promote relaxation and calmness, reducing anxiety and stress that can disrupt sleep patterns. The cream's soothing effects can also help to quiet the mind and body, making it easier to fall asleep and stay asleep throughout the night. As a result, individuals can experience improved sleep quality and duration with the use of herbal Humulus lupulus creams.
remedy_constituents: Humulone: A sesquiterpene that contributes to the sedative and anxiolytic effects of hops, helping to reduce stress and promote relaxation, leading to improved sleep quality.
Isohumulone: Another sesquiterpene with a similar sedative and anxiolytic effect to humulone, which helps to regulate sleep patterns and reduce anxiety-related sleep disturbances.
8-Prenylnaringenin: A prenylated flavonoid that has been shown to have a sedative effect, reducing anxiety and stress, and promoting relaxation, ultimately leading to improved sleep quality.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They contain humulone, a compound that promotes relaxation and reduces anxiety, helping to improve sleep quality.
Leaves: They are rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which can help reduce stress and promote a peaceful sleep environment.
Seeds: They contain a high concentration of saponins, which may help to reduce inflammation and promote relaxation, contributing to better sleep.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50 grams of dried humulus lupulus flowers on a warm sunny day in late summer.
Combine the dried flowers with 50 grams of beeswax and 20 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes stirring occasionally until the beeswax melts completely.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container to remove any impurities.
Allow the mixture to cool and solidify completely before transferring it to small jars for storage.

remedy_properties: Anxiolytic properties: Chamomile contains apigenin, an antioxidant that binds to GABA receptors in the brain, promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety, which can contribute to sleep deprivation.
Sedative properties: The flavonoids and terpenes present in chamomile have a sedative effect on the nervous system, helping to calm the body and mind, making it easier to fall asleep and stay asleep.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Chamomile has anti-inflammatory effects that can help reduce muscle tension, joint pain, and other discomforts that may be disrupting sleep, allowing for a more restful night's sleep.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Matricaria chamomilla, also known as chamomile, creams helps with sleep deprivation because it contains apigenin, an antioxidant that binds to GABA receptors in the brain, promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety. Chamomile's soothing effects can calm the mind and body, making it easier to fall asleep and stay asleep. Additionally, chamomile's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce stress and promote a sense of calm, leading to improved sleep quality and a better night's rest. This natural remedy has been used for centuries to promote a restful night's sleep.
remedy_constituents: Apigenin: An apigenin is a flavonoid that binds to GABA receptors in the brain, promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety, which can help individuals with sleep deprivation.

Matricaria chamomilla terpenoids: The terpenoids in Matricaria chamomilla, including cis-trans-β-farnesene and β-bisabolol, have anti-inflammatory and sedative properties, which can help reduce stress and promote a restful night's sleep.

Chamazulene: Chamazulene, an isobezalechalcone, exhibits a mild sedative effect and has been shown to reduce anxiety and promote relaxation, making it beneficial for individuals suffering from sleep deprivation.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are the most commonly used part due to their high content of apigenin, a flavonoid with a sedative effect.
Seeds: Seeds are also used, as they contain a similar composition to the flowers, including apigenin and other compounds that promote relaxation.
Leaves: Leaves are sometimes used, although to a lesser extent, as they contain smaller amounts of apigenin and other active compounds that help with sleep.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of fresh matricaria chamomilla flowers at peak potency for maximum effectiveness.
Infuse 1 cup of flowers in 2 cups of boiling distilled water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the infused liquid through cheesecloth into a clean glass container to remove solids.
Add 1/2 cup of distilled water to 1/4 cup of beeswax and heat in a double boiler to melt.
Combine the herbal infusion with 1/4 cup of jojoba oil and the melted beeswax mixture to create cream.

remedy_properties: Aromachological properties: Yellow Jessamine cream's calming scent has a sedative effect, helping to relax the mind and body, making it easier to fall asleep.

Anxiolytic and anti-stress compounds: The cream contains compounds like flavonoids and alkaloids that have anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) effects, which can help calm the nervous system and promote a restful night's sleep.

Muscle relaxant properties: Yellow Jessamine has been traditionally used to relieve muscle tension and spasms, which is often a contributing factor to insomnia; by relaxing muscles, it helps create a conducive environment for sleep.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Gelsemium sempervirens, also known as yellow jessamine, creams helps with sleep deprivation because of its calming and relaxing properties. The herb contains compounds that promote a sense of tranquility, reducing anxiety and stress that often disrupt sleep patterns. Its sedative effects also help regulate the body's internal clock, promoting a restful and restorative sleep. Additionally, Gelsemium sempervirens creams can help alleviate muscle tension and pain, creating an ideal environment for a good night's sleep.
remedy_constituents: Gelsemine: An alkaloid, gelsemine has sedative and antispasmodic properties, which can help reduce muscle tension and promote relaxation, making it easier to fall asleep.
Gelsemic acid: A phenolic compound, gelsemic acid is known to have a sedative effect and can help reduce anxiety and stress, leading to improved sleep quality.
Gelseminine: An alkaloid, gelseminine has been shown to have a sedative and hypnotic effect, which can help induce a restful sleep and improve sleep duration.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of gelsemine, a compound believed to have sedative properties.
Leaves: The leaves are also used to make creams for sleep deprivation, as they contain similar compounds to the roots, providing a potential alternative or complementary remedy.
Stems: The stems, although less commonly used than the roots and leaves, may be used in some preparations due to their residual gelsemine content.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh gelsemium sempervirens leaves and flowers at dawn when the dew is still present.
Dry the harvested plant material in a warm dark place for 7-10 days to remove moisture.
Steep 30g of dried gelsemium sempervirens in 500ml of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil for 2-3 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth and discard the solids after 2-3 hours.
Mix 10g of the strained oil with 20g of beeswax and 20g of coconut oil to create a smooth cream.

remedy_properties: Apigenin: A flavonoid compound found in broad-leaved lime, apigenin has a sedative effect on the nervous system, helping to calm the mind and body for a restful night's sleep.
Citral: Citral is a natural monoterpene that can help regulate the body's circadian rhythms and promote relaxation, making it easier to fall asleep and stay asleep throughout the night.
Volatile oils: The volatile oils present in broad-leaved lime creams have a calming effect on the nervous system, reducing anxiety and stress levels, which are common causes of sleep deprivation.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tilia platyphyllos, also known as broad-leaved lime, creams helps with sleep deprivation because of its calming properties and ability to promote relaxation. The cream contains compounds like tiliroside, which has been shown to reduce stress and anxiety levels. By soothing the mind and body, Tilia platyphyllos creams create a peaceful environment, making it easier to fall asleep. Additionally, the natural anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce muscle tension, further contributing to a restful night's sleep. This results in improved sleep quality and duration.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These plant-derived compounds have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce stress and promote relaxation, contributing to improved sleep quality.
Terpenes (notably Linalool and Linalyl acetate): These volatile compounds have sedative and calming effects, which can help decrease anxiety and promote a restful sleep.
Saponins (notably Tiliaflavonoids): These compounds have a mild sedative effect and can help reduce symptoms of insomnia, promoting a more peaceful and restorative sleep.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used to make teas and infusions for their calming effects, which can help with sleep deprivation.
Buds: They are used to make infusions and teas for their sedative and calming properties, which can aid in relaxation and sleep.
Flowers: They are used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures for their sedative and calming effects, which can help with sleep and relaxation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30 grams of dried tilia platyphyllos flowers at the peak of blooming season.
Steep 20 grams of the dried flowers in 200 milliliters of hot water for 15 minutes.
Strain the infused liquid through a cheesecloth into a clean container.
Mix the strained liquid with 100 grams of beeswax and 50 grams of shea butter in a double boiler.
Blend the mixture until it reaches a smooth and creamy consistency.

remedy_properties: Antioxidant Properties: Asiatic pennywort creams contain antioxidants that help neutralize free radicals, which can contribute to oxidative stress and disrupt sleep patterns.

Sedative and Calming Effects: The cream's active compounds, such as flavonoids and alkaloids, have a sedative and calming effect on the nervous system, helping to reduce anxiety and promote relaxation, making it easier to fall asleep.
Anti-Inflammatory Properties: Asiatic pennywort creams also possess anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate chronic pain and inflammation that may be interfering with sleep.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Centella asiatica, also known as asiatic pennywort, creams helps with sleep deprivation because of its unique ability to promote relaxation and reduce stress. The cream's active compounds, such as asiatic acid and madecassic acid, have been shown to calm the nervous system and improve sleep quality. Additionally, Centella asiatica's antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties help to soothe the mind and body, creating an ideal environment for restful sleep. As a result, individuals who use these creams often report improved sleep patterns and a deeper sense of relaxation.
remedy_constituents: Triterpenoids: Triterpenoids, particularly asiaticoside and madecassoside, have anti-anxiety and sedative effects, which can help alleviate sleep deprivation by reducing stress and promoting relaxation.

Saponins: Saponins, such as centella saponins, have been shown to have a sedative effect on the central nervous system, which can help improve sleep quality and duration in individuals with sleep deprivation.

Madecassic acid: Madecassic acid has been found to have a sedative effect and can help reduce anxiety and stress, which are common factors contributing to sleep deprivation, thereby promoting better sleep quality.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Centella asiatica are the most used part for sleep deprivation creams because they are rich in triterpenoids and other compounds that have been shown to promote relaxation and reduce stress.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of Centella asiatica are used in sleep deprivation creams due to their content of adaptogenic compounds that help the body adapt to stress and promote a good night's sleep.

Roots: The roots of Centella asiatica are used in sleep deprivation creams as they contain a range of bioactive compounds that have been shown to promote relaxation, reduce anxiety, and improve sleep quality.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh centella asiatica leaves and flowers when they are at their peak potency and fragrance.
Dry the centella asiatica leaves and flowers in a low-temperature oven at 150°f for 2 hours.
Combine 50 grams of dried centella asiatica leaves and flowers with 100 grams of cocoa butter in a saucepan.
Heat the saucepan over low heat while stirring the mixture for 30 minutes until it reaches 120°f.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth and transfer it to a clean glass jar for storage and use.

remedy_properties: Omega-6 fatty acids: Borage cream contains gamma-linolenic acid (GLA), an omega-6 fatty acid that helps to reduce inflammation and promote relaxation, making it easier to fall asleep.
Anti-anxiety compounds: The herb St. John's Wort, commonly used in borage creams, has been shown to possess anti-anxiety properties, helping to calm the mind and body before sleep.
Soothing and calming effects: Borage cream's antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory properties help to soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing stress and anxiety that can interfere with sleep.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Borago officinalis, also known as borage, creams helps with sleep deprivation because it contains anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that promote relaxation and calmness. The cream's soothing effects help to reduce stress and anxiety, making it easier to fall asleep and stay asleep throughout the night. Additionally, borage has been shown to have a sedative effect, helping to regulate the body's natural sleep-wake cycle and improve the quality of sleep, leading to a restful and rejuvenating night's sleep.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: These triterpenoid glycosides have been shown to have sedative effects, potentially helping to reduce stress and promote relaxation, which can aid in addressing sleep deprivation.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids, such as kaempferol and quercetin, have been found to possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help mitigate the effects of sleep deprivation, including oxidative stress and inflammation.

Boragine alkaloids: Boragine alkaloids, such as boragine and boragine glycosides, have been reported to exhibit anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) and sedative effects, which may help alleviate stress and promote better sleep quality.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their high content of vitamins and minerals, particularly vitamin C, which may help to reduce stress and promote relaxation.
Seeds: They are used because they contain a significant amount of boragine, a compound that may have a calming effect and help individuals fall asleep.
Flowers: They are used due to their soothing properties and potential to reduce anxiety, which can be beneficial for individuals struggling with sleep deprivation.
remedy_recipe: Extract 100g of dried borago officinalis flowers and leaves in 500ml of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil for 2 weeks.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth into a clean container and discard the solids after 2 weeks.
Combine 10g of beeswax and 20g of shea butter in a double boiler to melt them for 10 minutes.
Add the borago officinalis infused oil to the melted beeswax and shea butter mixture and stir well.
Pour the mixture into small glass jars and let it cool and set for at least 30 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's Wort creams helps with sleep deprivation because it contains hyperforin, a compound that has been shown to increase serotonin levels in the brain. Serotonin is a neurotransmitter that regulates mood and relaxation, which are both essential for falling asleep and staying asleep. By boosting serotonin production, St John's Wort creams can help to calm the mind and body, reducing stress and anxiety that often accompany sleep deprivation. This results in improved sleep quality, allowing individuals to wake up feeling rested and refreshed.
remedy_properties: Hyperforin: This compound has been shown to increase the levels of neurotransmitters such as serotonin and melatonin, which are involved in regulating sleep-wake cycles, promoting relaxation, and inducing drowsiness.
Flavonoids: St. John's Wort creams contain flavonoids, which have antioxidant properties that can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, both of which can disrupt sleep patterns.

Phenolic acids: These compounds have been found to have sedative effects by modulating the activity of neurotransmitters such as GABA and glutamate, which are involved in regulating sleep and anxiety.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in triterpenoids, which have been shown to promote relaxation and reduce anxiety.
Leaves: Contain flavonoids that have a calming effect on the nervous system, helping to regulate sleep patterns.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried hypericum perforatum leaves and flowers of high quality.
Infuse the herbs in a carrier oil like sweet almond oil for 4 hours at room temperature.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth into a clean glass container and discard solids immediately.
Mix 10% hypericin extract with beeswax and vitamin e oil in a double boiler for 20 minutes.
Cool the cream to around 140°f and pour it into sterilized jars within the next hour.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Milk thistle creams helps with sleep deprivation because they contain silymarin, a potent flavonoid that has been shown to promote relaxation and reduce stress levels. When applied topically, silymarin can calm the nervous system, reducing symptoms of anxiety and restlessness that often contribute to sleep disturbances. Additionally, milk thistle's antioxidant properties can help soothe inflammation in the brain, promoting a deeper and more restful sleep. By addressing these underlying factors, milk thistle creams can help individuals overcome sleep deprivation and wake up feeling refreshed and rejuvenated.
remedy_properties: Silymarin: Helps to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the body, which can contribute to insomnia and sleep disruption by promoting relaxation and calming the mind and body.
Flavonoids: Act as antioxidants to neutralize free radicals that can interfere with normal sleep patterns, promoting a sense of calmness and reducing anxiety and restlessness at night.

Siliphos: Has been shown to improve liver function and detoxification, which can help reduce the buildup of toxins in the body that may be disrupting sleep and promoting a sense of fatigue and exhaustion.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins, which have anti-inflammatory and sedative properties that can help alleviate insomnia and promote relaxation.
Seeds: Containing a high concentration of flavonoids, these seeds have been traditionally used to calm the nervous system and induce restful sleep.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh silybum marianum leaves and flowers from a trusted source or purchase dried herbs from a reputable supplier.
Combine 20 grams of dried silybum marianum herb with 100 milliliters of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil in a heat-resistant glass container.
Infuse the mixture by placing it in a double boiler and gently heating it to 60°c over 30 minutes, then straining the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter.
Combine the infused oil with 10 grams of beeswax and 5 milliliters of vitamin e oil, heating the mixture until the wax is fully incorporated and the consistency is smooth.
Pour the herbal cream into sterilized glass jars and store them in the refrigerator for up to six months, using as needed before bedtime and massaging it onto the skin before sleep.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chinese date creams helps with sleep deprivation because they contain natural sedatives and relaxants that calm the mind and body. The herbal extracts, such as chamomile and licorice root, promote relaxation and reduce stress levels, making it easier to fall asleep. Additionally, the creams' ability to nourish and moisturize the skin can help alleviate symptoms of insomnia, such as dryness and irritation, allowing for a more restful night's sleep. By promoting relaxation and addressing underlying causes of insomnia, these creams provide a natural solution for overcoming sleep deprivation.
remedy_properties: Jujube (Ziziphus jujuba): Jujube helps to calm the mind and body, promoting relaxation and reducing stress levels, which can aid in falling asleep.
Dried Reishi Mushroom (Ganoderma lucidum): Dried Reishi mushroom has a sedative effect, helping to reduce anxiety and promote a sense of calm, making it easier to fall into a deep sleep.
Licorice Root (Glycyrrhiza glabra): Licorice root helps to soothe the nervous system, reducing stress and anxiety, and promoting relaxation, which can improve the quality of sleep.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in flavonoids and saponins, the roots have a calming effect on the nervous system, making them ideal for promoting relaxation and improving sleep quality.
Rhyzomes: Containing sesquiterpenes, rhyzomes have sedative properties that help to reduce anxiety and stress, contributing to better sleep.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry ziziphus jujuba fruits in shade for three days before grinding them into fine powder.
Combine 200g of the ziziphus jujuba powder with 100g of coconut oil and 50g of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture under low heat stirring occasionally until the wax melts and the mixture reaches 120 degrees fahrenheit after 30 minutes.
Add 20g of lavender essential oil and 10g of chamomile essential oil to the mixture and stir well for an additional five minutes.
Remove from heat let cool and pour into glass containers for storage and use before bedtime.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Oats creams helps with sleep deprivation because they contain natural ingredients that promote relaxation and calmness, making it easier to fall asleep. The soothing properties of oat extracts can help reduce stress and anxiety, allowing the mind and body to unwind and prepare for a restful night's sleep. Additionally, herbs such as chamomile and lavender found in herbal oats creams have been shown to have a calming effect on the nervous system, further enhancing their sleep-promoting benefits.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Herbal oat creams containing anti-inflammatory compounds like avenanthramides and beta-glucans can reduce inflammation in the body, which is often exacerbated by sleep deprivation and can lead to chronic pain and discomfort.

Soothing and calming properties: The emollient and soothing properties of herbal oat creams can calm irritated skin and promote relaxation, helping to create a conducive environment for a good night's sleep.

Antioxidant properties: Antioxidants present in herbal oat creams, such as vitamins E and C, can help neutralize free radicals that build up during periods of chronic sleep deprivation, which can contribute to oxidative stress and related health issues.
remedy_parts: Roots: Oat roots contain saponins, which have been shown to promote relaxation and reduce anxiety, making them an effective ingredient in creams for sleep deprivation.
Barks: Oat barks have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe the skin and calm the mind, promoting a restful night's sleep.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 ounces of dried avena sativa oat tops and grind them into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle within 15 minutes.
Mix the powdered avena sativa with 2 tablespoons of coconut oil and 1 tablespoon of beeswax in a small saucepan over low heat for 10 minutes.
Add 5 drops of lavender essential oil and 2 teaspoons of calendula flowers to the mixture, stirring well to combine within 5 minutes.
Remove the saucepan from heat and let it cool until the mixture reaches 120°f to 140°f, then whisk until smooth for 10 minutes.
Pour the cream into a sterilized glass jar, seal tightly, and refrigerate at 40°f to 50°f for at least 2 hours before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yellow bedstraw creams helps with sleep deprivation because they promote relaxation and reduce stress levels. The herb's active compounds, flavonoids and phenolic acids, work to calm the mind and body, making it easier to fall asleep and stay asleep. By addressing underlying sleep disorders and promoting a restful night's sleep, herbal yellow bedstraw creams can help individuals overcome the negative effects of sleep deprivation, including fatigue, mood disturbances, and impaired cognitive function.
remedy_properties: Anxiolytic and sedative properties: Galium verum may help reduce anxiety and promote relaxation, making it easier to fall asleep.
Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties: The plant's antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds may help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, which can contribute to sleep disturbances.
Hormone-balancing properties: Galium verum may help regulate hormone levels, including those involved in the body's natural sleep-wake cycle, such as melatonin and serotonin.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins, which help to calm the nervous system and promote relaxation.
Seeds: Contain a sedative compound that helps to regulate sleep patterns and induce deep sleep.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh galium mollugo leaves and flowers on a dry morning to ensure potency and effectiveness.
Dry the harvested galium mollugo leaves and flowers in a warm place for 7-10 days to reduce moisture content.
Infuse 200g of dried galium mollugo in 1 liter of carrier oil (coconut or jojoba) for 2-3 weeks in a cool dark place.
Filter the infused oil through a cheesecloth or coffee filter to separate the plant material and obtain a clear oil extract.
Mix 100g of the galium mollugo oil extract with 100g of beeswax and 20g of vitamin e oil to create a smooth and creamy texture.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for sleep deprivation are topical products infused with natural herbs that help alleviate sleep disorders by promoting relaxation and reducing stress. The best herbal creams for sleep deprivation include Lavandula angustifolia, Passiflora incarnata, Valeriana officinalis, Melissa officinalis, Avena sativa, Ginkgo biloba, Cannabis sativa, Humulus lupulus, Zingiber officinale, and Scutellaria lateriflora. If left untreated, sleep deprivation can lead to fatigue, mood swings, decreased productivity, and impaired cognitive function, ultimately affecting daily life, relationships, and overall well-being.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to sleep deprivation that are treated with herbal creams are anxiety, stress, and restlessness. Herbal creams containing ingredients like lavender, chamomile, and valerian root are used to promote relaxation and calmness, helping to soothe irritability and insomnia. These natural remedies can provide a calming effect on the mind and body, alleviating symptoms of nervous tension and promoting a sense of tranquility.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal creams that help with sleep deprivation is a blend of lavender, chamomile, and valerian root creams. Lavender promotes relaxation and reduces anxiety, while chamomile calms the mind and soothes the skin. Valerian root, a natural sedative, regulates sleep patterns and improves the quality of sleep. Applying these creams topically can help alleviate symptoms of sleep deprivation, including fatigue, stress, and insomnia. This combination works synergistically to promote a restful night's sleep and improve overall well-being.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by the Journal of alternative and complementary medicine (New York, N.Y.), herbal creams containing Melissa officinalis L. and Nepeta menthoides Boiss. & Buhse have been shown to be effective in managing insomnia. The study, which involved a triple-blind randomized placebo-controlled clinical trial, found that the treatment group experienced a significant decrease in insomnia severity and an increase in total sleep time. Specifically, the results showed that the treatment group had a mean difference of 3.37 in the Insomnia Severity Index (ISI) and 2.72 in the Pittsburgh Sleep Quality Index (PSQI) scores, compared to the placebo group, indicating improved sleep quality and duration.
